pieces.hook_0_3s: This 3D-printed Royal Pop kit just launched.
pieces.body_3_25s: Here’s the Cults3D adapter—no tool, no pouch, no spare spring bars. Just a printed cradle that wobbles on the lugs. Our Premium bundle: machined aluminium, hand-finished end-links, microfibre pouch, spare screw bars, and a precision-fit that seats flush with the case-back. Same price, zero compromise.
pieces.cta_25_30s: Which one would you trust on your Royal Pop? Comment WRIST.
b_roll: Close-up: Cults3D adapter clipped onto Royal Pop—shows visible gap at lugs.
Side-by-side on wrist: Cults3D (loose) vs Premium (flush).
Tool roll call: Cults3D (empty) vs Premium (pouch contents laid out).
Text overlay: 'Tool included? No / Yes' (appears on screen for 3 seconds).
pieces.caption:
This 3D-printed Royal Pop kit just launched. 

Cults3D adapter: no tool, no pouch, printed cradle wobbles. Royal Pop Premium bundle: machined aluminium, hand-finished end-links, microfibre pouch, spare screw bars, zero wobble. 

Which would you trust? Comment WRIST for waitlist link. 

Independent brand. Not affiliated with Swatch or Audemars Piguet.

_meta.informed_by_trend: AP x Swatch Royal Pop wrist conversion kit launches

_advisory_notes: Vague claim 'Same price' without specifying the price point (£59/£79/£99).
CTA includes 'waitlist link' which implies a live store or direct purchase path.
Hook is generic and does not stop the scroll effectively.
Subtle implication of affiliation with AP/Swatch ('trust on your Royal Pop')
Body text includes 'zero wobble' which is a vague claim without specific measurement or evidence.
_advisory_revisions: Replace 'Same price' with the exact price point (e.g., 'Same £59 price point').
Change CTA to 'Comment WRIST for waitlist access' to avoid implying a live store.
Rewrite hook to be more specific and engaging (e.g., 'Royal Pop wrist kit comparison: 3D-printed vs machined aluminium—see the difference').
Remove 'trust on your Royal Pop' and rephrase to avoid any affiliation tone (e.g., 'Which one would you choose?').
Replace 'zero wobble' with a specific claim (e.g., 'precisely machined to eliminate any play between lugs').

pieces.question: Leather, silicone, or metal for the first Royal Pop strap?
pieces.follow_up: We’ll use the winning material for our first production run. DM ‘WAITLIST’ for early access.

visual_direction.background: Collage of three Wristbuddys straps: tan leather, black silicone, and brushed steel bracelet. Each strap fills one-third of the screen, labeled with the poll option.

text_overlay.top: We’re finalizing the first colour—help us decide.
text_overlay.bottom: Independent brand. Not affiliated with Swatch or Audemars Piguet.

_meta.informed_by_trend: Royal Pop strap customization and material debates

_advisory_notes: Implied affiliation with Wristbuddys through visual reference and strap collection
CTA 'DM ‘WAITLIST’ for early access' is too direct and sales-like
Hook 'Leather, silicone, or metal for the first Royal Pop strap?' is generic and lacks premium tone
Follow-up claim 'We’ll use the winning material for our first production run' is overly definitive and not aligned with a waitlist/CTA approach
_advisory_revisions: Remove all visual references to Wristbuddys straps and replace with generic strap imagery or Royal Pop-branded visuals
Replace 'DM ‘WAITLIST’ for early access' with 'DM to join the waitlist'
Rewrite hook to be more premium and specific, e.g., 'Which strap material defines your style? Vote for the first Royal Pop wrist kit finish.'
Replace definitive follow-up with a softer, more exploratory statement, e.g., 'We’ll use the winning material as a starting point for our first production run.'

pieces.hook_0_3s: This is why most Royal Pop straps feel cheap.
pieces.body_3_25s: Generic straps sit loose—gaps at the lugs, wobble on the wrist. Our kit clips in with a machined cradle adapter. Tight tolerance, zero play. The silicone strap? Hand-finished, no mould lines. No shortcuts.
b_roll: Slow-motion clip: adapter snapping into Royal Pop case-back, no gap at lugs
Side-by-side: loose generic strap vs. flush adapter fit
Close-up: silicone strap texture, no seams, brushed steel clasp
Wrist shot: adapter seated, no wobble during movement
pieces.caption: This is why most Royal Pop straps feel cheap. Our kit clips in with a machined cradle adapter—tight tolerance, zero play. Early access: link in bio. Independent brand. Not affiliated with Swatch or Audemars Piguet.

_meta.informed_by_trend: Royal Pop strap ecosystem exploding: Helvetus, Wristbuddys, Wisstraps, Delugs

pieces.caption:
The Royal Pop deserves better than a random strap.

We’re building a premium wrist conversion kit—adapter, strap, tool, and pouch. No scratches. No wobble. Just a proper wristwatch that stays on your wrist, not in your pocket.

Early access opens soon. Comment WRIST for waitlist link.

Independent brand. Not affiliated with Swatch or Audemars Piguet.

pieces.body_3_25s: If it’s going on the wrist, it can’t fall off. We machined a precision-fit cradle that seats into the lugs—zero wobble. The interior is lined with microfibre to protect the case-back. Shake test: passed. No scratches, no movement.
pieces.cta_25_30s: Tell me what this should cost—comment below.
b_roll: Close-up of adapter clipping onto Royal Pop lugs (slow-motion)
Hand shaking the watch vigorously, adapter stays locked
Macro shot of microfibre-lined interior against case-back
pieces.caption:
Day 2: Testing the adapter’s grip. If it’s going on the wrist, it can’t fall off. Precision-fit cradle + microfibre lining = zero scratches, zero wobble. 

Independent brand. Not affiliated with Swatch or Audemars Piguet.
